ul.li: Shared Lock Strategy: By wrapping the entire query batch in a single RAII read-lock guard, we eliminate the O(N) atomic overhead of locking/unlocking per individual query.
Memory Alignment: Vectors are 64-byte aligned (0x40 boundary) to ensure single-cycle cache line fills and prevent "split-load" penalties.
Temporal Locality: As Query 1 iterates through the segment, the hardware prefetcher populates L2/L3. Query 2 (running in the same batch) finds data already in the high-speed cache.
SIMD Layout: Padding at 0x0C00 ensures the subsequent Vec 1 Header starts on a clean cache line boundary.

g.title: Acquired once per batch to prevent ref-count churn
Cache Hot: Reuses vectors from L2/L3
Acquired once per batch to prevent ref-count churn
Cache Hot: Reuses vectors from L2/L3
